disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Reproducible intermittent claudication - calf cramp after ~50 m of walking, fully relieved by 5-10 minutes of rest
trigger_question_examples: When exactly does the pain come on, and what makes it go away?
How far can you walk before it starts?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: After about 50 metres my calves cramp up and I have to stop. If I rest for 5-10 minutes it goes completely, then it comes back when I walk again.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Acute worsening of the claudication over the past week
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's been there for months but it's got noticeably worse this past week - I can't get as far now.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No rest pain, no ulceration, and no sudden pallor, coldness or numbness of the foot (no acute limb ischaemia)
trigger_question_examples: Do you get pain in the leg at rest, especially at night?
Has the foot ever suddenly gone pale, cold, or numb?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, it only hurts when I walk - no pain at rest, no ulcers, and my foot hasn't suddenly gone cold or numb.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No unilateral swelling, redness or a tender cord (arguing against DVT); pain is bilateral and strictly exertional
trigger_question_examples: Is one leg swollen, red, or tender to touch?
Is the pain constant or only when you walk?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No swelling or redness, and it's both legs - only when I walk, never just sitting still.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Longstanding avoidance of medical care with sedentary lifestyle and processed diet (very high cardiovascular risk, no current medications)
trigger_question_examples: When did you last see a doctor or have your cholesterol checked?
Are you on any regular medications?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Honestly, I've avoided doctors for years. I'm not on anything, and my diet's pretty poor.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Is this a blood clot? Should I be rushing to hospital tonight?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Should reassure that exertional cramp relieved by rest, in both legs with no swelling, is more like poor circulation (claudication) than a clot, while explaining it still needs prompt vascular assessment. Should NOT dismiss it, but should also avoid unnecessary emergency alarm - and must clearly state the warning signs (constant rest pain, a pale/cold/numb foot) that WOULD need the emergency department.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: So do I just need to rest my legs and it'll get better?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Should explain that rest alone is not the treatment - this needs prompt vascular assessment (pulse checks/ABI), starting an antiplatelet and cholesterol-lowering therapy, risk-factor changes, and a structured walking programme, because the same plaque threatens the heart and brain.

deflection_behaviours.description: Patient has avoided doctors for years and may want to keep avoiding formal assessment - tests whether the AI Doctor secures prompt vascular follow-up and risk-factor treatment rather than accepting 'just rest'.
deflection_behaviours.deflection_text_template: I'd rather just rest it and see how it goes, I don't like doctors.
